ocr.text: The assessment of hypocalcemia detection in patients is critical for optimizing treatment strategies and improving patient
outcomes. The accompanying data table presents an analysis of various detection methods, highlighting key metrics such as the
number of patients (N) and the corresponding detection percentages (%). Notably, the table indicates that out of 121 patients,
43.2% were accurately identified as having hypocalcemia, underscoring the efficacy of the employed detection methodologies.
The incremental yield metrics also provide valuable insights; specifically, the incremental yield over the baseline detection
method (D) reveals a significant improvement in detection rates, with analysis showing an increase in the number of cases
detected. Furthermore, the rule description column illustrates that the medication entity is accurately detected, with both class
and boundary divisions correctly applied. This comprehensive evaluation not only emphasizes the importance of precise
diagnostic tools but also sets the stage for further research aimed at enhancing detection rates and ensuring timely interventions
for patients suffering from hypocalcemia.
Electroyte imbalance  Cases
N  Percent (%)
Hypocalcemia  47  23.38
Hypomagnesemia  28  13.93
Hypokalemia  18  8.96
Hyperkalemia  0  0
Score  Rule description
1  Medication entity is accurately detected, and divisions of class and boundary are both correct
0.8  Only one error is detected at the start position of the ME boundary
0.6  Only one error is detected at the end position of the ME boundary
0.4  Two errors are detected at the start and end positions of ME boundaries, respectively
0  ME is not detected, or the detected phrase is not a drug name entity annotated in the gold standard
Case definition of one smear  Case definition of two smears
N detected (% of all patients)  Incremental yield over D alone: N (%; 95% CI)  N detected (% of all patients)  Incremental yield over D alone: N (%; 95% CI)
